headline: TASK-075 is the live v6.7 frontier after 6.6.0.

current_state.operating_goal: Advance v6.7 Verifiable Governance: make missing archmap impact dispositions, self-attested review provenance, and low-independence evidence machine-detectable so release sign-off can fail on structural self-attestation instead of trusting maker claims.
current_state.next_value_slice: Implement TASK-075 release-state-audit integration: make release-state-audit consume archmap-impact-audit, review-provenance-audit, and evidence-independence-audit, and fill empty command help inputs/outputs where still missing. Verification gate: release-state-audit fails when any v6.7 governance audit fails; command help benchmark remains green; unit tests pass. Stop condition: integration only; no new audit families. QIF boundary: integrated audit pass is structural/runtime release-readiness evidence, not proof of semantic product quality.

primary_frontier_task.title: v6.7 frontier: integrate governance audits into release sign-off

visible_state.speech_bubble: I need council review before I should execute.
visible_state.next_action: Submit the gate packet to council review before actor execution.

why_now.summary: TASK-075 is the best current frontier because it matches the live post-release goal surface and no stronger truth conflict is active.
reasons: The active release baseline is 6.6.0 and the runtime is now targeting v6.7.
The current operating goal and next value slice both reinforce TASK-075 as the next bounded operating move.
